rationale: X better covers both ground-truth threads (test-coverage adequacy and the CuMemTag enum design), giving it stronger recall, and its default-level=2 finding gestures at the same over-strictness the LATENT GAP CHECK flags. Y misses the regression-test thread entirely but lands a much sharper, evidence-backed gap hit: it names specific existing tests (including test_diffusion_vram_lifecycle_audit, visible right in the diff context) that call sleep(level=2)+wake_up() and would now break, plus catches a genuine separate blocker (a leftover _is_sleeping assignment in another test file that would raise AttributeError) that X never finds. Both reviews are highly actionable with concrete file/line fixes; Y edges ahead on precision and on the depth/concreteness of its gap-check evidence, giving it a slight overall edge despite weaker thread recall.
